data.body:
CORRECTION / refinement (looked at the STORED cause; did NOT re-run — re-running only adds a sample, not a cause, and assumes a replicability we can't justify from the record):

1) Stored cause (ad9f FAIL COMPLETION_VERDICT @ 1784840651): both unmet findings' own `detail` acknowledge the IMPLEMENTATION exists (cite _cli/__init__.py:406-407, _init.py ensure_store_mounted_best_effort, _NO_AUTO_MOUNT line 42) and mark the criterion unmet ONLY for lack of a confirmed TEST. PASS @ 1784840960 credited the same two. So the verifier's own recorded reasoning localizes the flip to TEST-EXISTENCE crediting, not code assessment.

2) SIDECAR DIAGNOSABILITY GAP (new, concrete): diffing the two full records — same model (anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6), same runner (pydantic_ai), material_fingerprint=None in BOTH, coverage={} in BOTH. The COMPLETION_VERDICT sidecar does NOT persist the verified head_sha / verified_at_sha, nor a populated coverage, nor the input context. Therefore the record ALONE cannot prove the two runs had identical input — so 'it is a replicable flake' is not something the sidecar justifies. This is itself a defect: the sidecar is too slim to diagnose the verifier's own non-determinism. Fix: record the verify-sha, the resolved input snapshot id, model settings (temp/seed), and per-criterion evidence in the sidecar (this is the 'record WHY structurally' item, now with a concrete missing-field list).

3) Input-identity here is established by TIMELINE, not the record: both were ad9f closes from the same worktree at origin/main ~5min apart with no commits/HEAD movement between (one reset --hard origin/main, nothing else), and the 4de6 fix pins HEAD at entry, so both closes pinned the same commit. Identical input + flipped verdict => genuine non-determinism on test-existence.

Net: the mitigations still hold, and (2) is a prerequisite — until the sidecar records the verify-sha + inputs + decoding settings, non-determinism cannot be distinguished from input drift purely from stored data.
